items.title: This state agency was created to investigate Texas universities. How that works is a mystery.
items.desc: Office of the Ombudsman has no written policies on how to investigate allegations that education laws are being broken, even though it’s been accepting complaints for three months.

items.title: South Texas officials dismissed from woman’s wrongful arrest lawsuit in controversial abortion case
items.desc: Starr County remains the sole defendant in a suit seeking $1 million in damages filed by Lizelle Gonzalez, who accused local officials of malicious prosecution and conspiracy.

items.title: Texas attorney general’s office under scrutiny for letting donors use hotel room bookings
items.desc: The attorney general’s office reallocated taxpayer-funded rooms to donors and other private citizens, some of whom initially failed to cover the cost of the stay.

items.title: At least 175 measles cases reported in Texas this year, most of them in West Texas Detention Facility
items.desc: Texas has added 28 more measles cases in the last two weeks. Most infections so far this year have occurred inside West Texas Detention Facility in Hudspeth County.

items.title: This East Texas city is intentionally attracting people with kids amid growth boom
items.desc: Tyler has spent the last decade remaking its parks and sidewalks to allure families. At the same time, a constellation of groups are helping parents take care of their families.

items.title: Turkey Weighs Raising Energy Prices
items.desc: Turkey is weighing whether to raise electricity and natural gas prices, according to people familiar with the matter.

items.title: DOI Says USA Energy Production Hit Record Levels in 2025
items.desc: 'American energy leadership is a cornerstone of our economic strength and national security', Secretary of the Interior Doug Burgum said.

items.title: TotalEnergies, Masdar Form $2.2B Renewables JV in Asia
items.desc: Masdar and TotalEnergies announced an agreement to create a 50-50 joint venture to consolidate their onshore renewable energy portfolio in nine Asian countries.

items.title: Why Has the WTI Oil Price Surpassed Brent?
items.desc: Why has the West Texas Intermediate (WTI) oil price surpassed Brent?

items.title: Manila Says Iran Assured Passage for Ships Carrying Energy for Philippines
items.desc: 'Iran will allow the safe, unhindered and expeditious passage through the Strait of Hormuz of Philippine-flagged vessels, energy sources and all Filipino seafarers', the Philippine Foreign Affairs Department said.

items.title: DOE’s Section 202(c) Emergency Orders Since May 2025: 43 and Counting

items.title: Investment Group Acquires Stake in TotalEnergies’ German Energy Storage Portfolio

items.title: Scientists urge EPA not to weaken ethylene oxide emissions standards
items.desc: Scientists, clinicians and residents of communities near medical device sterilization plants called for the EPA not to weaken its oversight of ethylene oxide in a public hearing.

items.title: Adult Swim SMALLS Debuts 'Bad Luck Buck: Pop Up' Short from Todd Redner
items.desc: A new installment just dropped in the 'Bad Luck Buck' series following on the heels of the recent release of 'Bad Luck Buck: Unfriended.'

items.title: Crystal City, Rakia Media and Nate Hopper to Develop 'Tiny T. Rex' Animated Series
items.desc: The bestselling children’s book franchise is being adapted into preschool animation and other IP projects.

items.title: Hasbro Appoints Adam Redfern as 'Peppa Pig' Showrunner
items.desc: The Emmy and BAFTA-winning writer and producer will oversee creative direction on the hit show as the company positions the long-running preschool series for the next phase of its storytelling evolution.

items.title: MeTV Toons Sets ‘Easter Eggstravaganza’ Programming Block for April 5
items.desc: The network will air a full day of classic animated holiday specials featuring Bugs Bunny, Yogi Bear, and the Smurfs, among others.
